chapter_title.en: Chapter on Zihar (declaring one's Wife Unlawful Like a mother)

text: 4841 - وَ رَوَى أَيُّوبُ بْنُ نُوحٍ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ عَنِ اِبْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلْمُظَاهِرُ إِذَا صَامَ شَهْراً وَ صَامَ مِنَ اَلشَّهْرِ اَلْآخَرِ يَوْماً فَقَدْ وَاصَلَ فَإِنْ شَاءَ فَلْيَقْضِ مُتَفَرِّقاً وَ إِنْ شَاءَ فَلْيُعْطِ لِكُلِّ يَوْمٍ مُدّاً مِنْ طَعَامٍ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.4841 - Ayyub ibn Nuh narrated from Safwan, from Ibn Uyaynah, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "If a man who has performed ẓihar fasts for one full month and then fasts a day from the second month, he has fulfilled the condition of consecutive fasting. After that, if he wishes, he may make up the remaining fasts in a non-consecutive manner, or if he wishes, he may give a mudd (a specific measure) of food for each remaining day."
